title: Team-EV-Turkey
text:
This is our project webpage.
Please stay tuned to follow our project activities.
Welcome to the project webpage of Team-EV-Turkey! This website hosts our reproducible workflow and interactive findings for the EMU430 Data Analytics course.

title: Team-EV-Turkey
text:
The Core Question
Is Turkey’s EV charging infrastructure growing fast enough to support the rise of electric vehicles, and which provinces may face infrastructure bottlenecks by 2027?

title: Team-EV-Turkey
text:
Presentation Roadmap

The Context: Why EVs and why now?
The Engine: How we cleaned the EPDK provincial data (Fuzzy Matching).
The Forecast: Projecting the 2027 EV landscape (Log-Linear Models).
The Solution: Identifying “Investment Hot Spots” and infrastructure gaps.

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
Important🚀 Key Executive Takeaways




Exponential Growth: Adoption of EVs in Turkey is proceeding exponentially, not linearly. The market is accelerating faster than traditional forecasts.
Investment Bottlenecks: Based on our 2027 estimates, there will be a critical shortage of charging stations not only in urban centers but particularly in transit-route provinces.
Actionable Prioritization: At a benchmark of “50 EVs per station,” immediate capital injection is required in the critical (red) zones mapped below to sustain Turkey’s EV targets.

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
1. 📊 Correlation Reality Check
Before projecting the future, we tested the current relationship between electric vehicles and charging infrastructure.



--- EVIDENCE ---


Correlation between EV count and station count: 0.9826 



Insight: A high correlation proves that current station investments reactively follow market demand. Our goal is to shift this from reactive to proactive.

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
2. 📈 Market Growth Trend (Top 5 Hubs)
When examining the time-series growth chart of the top 5 provinces, the exponential curve is undeniable.

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
3. 🎯 2027 Projections & Investment Gaps
We forecasted the 2027 EV count using a Log-Linear Model and identified provincial investment gaps based on an optimal benchmark of 50 EVs per station.







Province Tier
Action Required by 2027




🔴 Critical Risk
Immediate capital injection; infrastructure is severely lagging.


🟠 High Risk
Infrastructure expansion must double to meet forecasted demand.


🟢 Adequate
Current growth pace is sufficient.

title: Insights & 2027 Projections
text:
4. 🗺️ Turkey 2027: The Interactive Heatmap
This is where the investments must go. The static map shows the risk distribution, while the Interactive Map below calculates the exact USD required to close the gap in each province (assuming a $50k cost per DC fast charger).






Note



Hover Instructions: Use your mouse to explore the interactive map below to view specific 2027 EV forecasts, missing stations, and required capital per province.

title: Data Processing & Methodology
text:
To ensure transparency and reliability, the foundational dataset for our project was compiled from a unified official source:

EPDK (Energy Market Regulatory Authority): Provincial-level data containing the number of registered electric vehicles and the geographical distribution of charging stations (sockets).

title: Data Processing & Methodology
text:
To ensure transparency and reliability, the foundational dataset for our project was compiled from a unified official source:

EPDK (Energy Market Regulatory Authority): Provincial-level data containing the number of registered electric vehicles and the geographical distribution of charging stations (sockets).

title: Data Processing & Methodology
text:
🛠️ Preprocessing & “Fuzzy Matching”
The most significant challenge we faced while merging raw datasets was the formatting of city names (e.g., encoding errors, typos, HTML tags).
To solve this without introducing human error, we built a “City Repair” Engine:

The Problem: Corrupted data entries like “Afyonkara” or “Istanbul ”.
The Solution: Fuzzy Matching Algorithm.
The Tool: The stringdist package in R.







TipTechnical Insight



Every provincial entry was automatically cross-referenced and corrected against an official benchmark list of 81 provinces using a maximum distance metric (amatch). This ensured 100% data integrity before modeling.


The following is our reproducible code pipeline for cleaning the datasets:

text:
📦 Final Dataset
Our fully processed, cleaned, and merged dataset is packed in .RData format for analysis and modeling.
